_comment: IntelliJ BYOK bolt-on example params. Copy to main.parameters.json, fill in, then run deploy.ps1/deploy.sh (recommended — it preflights the inputs and passes any secret inline). appInsightsName + appInsightsResourceGroup point at an EXISTING Application Insights in the SAME subscription as APIM (the customer's, the pilot's, or any provided one) — no App Insights is created and the template reads its connection string itself. foundryApiKey is the key the bolt-on uses to reach the EXISTING Foundry backend (api-key auth, NOT managed identity — unlike the standard BYOK deployment); supply it via deploy.ps1/deploy.sh -FoundryApiKey (never in this file) UNLESS that backend already carries its own credential — empty when required => Foundry 401. vmAdminSshPublicKey is the operator's SSH public key (password auth is disabled). AUTO-ROUTE: autoRouteSentinel defaults to 'auto' and tiers requests — short non-coding prompts to the cheap mini deployment, coding/long prompts to the full deployment — but only ACTIVATES once autoRouteMiniDeployment + autoRouteFullDeployment name real Foundry deployments (leave them blank to disable; clients then just pass a specific model). proxyImageId (optional) is a pre-baked Compute Gallery image for air-gapped subnets — see README §9.
